title: Amauropelta harrisii
taxonomy: Amauropelta harrisii is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which is a group of ferns native to North and South America. This species is further classified as a member of the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns with a distinctive leaf structure. The classification of Amauropelta harrisii is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Blechnales, Family: Thelypteridaceae, Genus: Amauropelta, and Species: A. harrisii. The classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of Amauropelta harrisii within the fern family.

intro: Amauropelta harrisii, also known as Harris' maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
